reviews.review_msg: Dr. Rosenthal was a really fun prof to have for a phil class (sorry phil majors, jst not my type of thing haha). Would def take this class or any other class with her again. The in class writes are marked fairly, the final exam is very fair as well + you get a cheatsheet!

One of the best phil profs at SFU. Her lectures are so well-organized that you could get away with not doing the readings and still understand the material perfectly fine. Also a very reasonable marker who gives great feedback. The world would be a better place if more profs like Chelsea existed.

Chelsea is the sweetest most accommodating prof I have ever had. She cares so much about her students learning and comfort. Her lectures are super helpful as she is an expert at synthesizing the key points from a reading, putting them into clear wording, and then engaging students in relevant discussion. And she always selects interesting readings.

Chelsea is a very good professor. Her lectures are very beneficial in understanding the course material. However 90% of the course is based off three exams (two in-class writes and a final). Strong performance is mandatory in these exams. Overall, I would recommend Chelsea.
She's very sweet and approachable, and the course was clear and well organized. We were allowed a double-sided, typed sheet, which was helpful.

Chelsea was a good professor! Her lectures were based on analyzing the readings, so it would get a bit boring at times though. The TA's grading felt subjective, so I would say be wary of that. Final exam was pretty easy and a cheat sheet was allowed. Overall pretty easy class, your grade just fully depends on your TA's marking style.

Chelsa is very approachable and is a kind person. The lectures mainly follow the slides, so the information is available online. Assignments can feel subjective depending on the TA, but the final exam is fair and straightforward with a cheat sheet. As long as you understand the key concepts and can make good objections, you will do really well.
2 in class essays, final, and participation. not the greatest class and you don't need to go to lecture at all. Her and the TAs were super nice but just not my fav class

Very nice prof, lectures can get repetitive and boring though. There's quite a few readings each week, but it's not an unbearable amount. Overall a good course to take if you need credits, however there isn't much to be graded on besides 2 in class assignments and the final.

Pretty much just read off of the slides without adding much else. Claims to mark like there's no right answer but is expecting a very specific one. Lectures felt like they covered much of the same content from week to week and it would have been more efficient to condense the ideas and use less readings. 3 readings a week is quite a bit
Chelsea was nice and gave pretty good lectures, TAs were okay, wouldn't take the class again bc the grading is dumb and they also don't really give good feedback

I personally wouldn't take this course as a GPA booster. It is advertised at the beginning of class as being about questions with no right or wrong answer, however, if you don't answer the way the TA wants then you'll get marks deducted. Prof also only reads off the slides for the whole lecture.

genuinely a great prof! kinda long readings but i enjoyed the content. not super necessary to read ahead because she covers the readings and context in depth. also offers lectures on zoom which is a plus. definitely recommend!!

Super boring course, took it for credit but the professor is nice enough. She didn't have office hours which I didn't like, had to go to my TA for everything. Slides are pretty clear and are posted on canvas, pretty much everything was covered there so no need to attend lectures
Professor Rosenthal is committed to providing an excellent learning environment for her students. Recommended.

Dr. Rosenthal is wonderful! She is super understanding and caring and encourages everyone's perspectives which makes participation feel a lot less daunting. She is a very clear lecturer and is really helpful in office hours in developing your ideas. She is also just an overall great person. Would definitely recommend taking a class with her!

Cares very much about her students' success and is understanding about extenuating circumstances. Lecture content and homework is easily accessible and workload itself is easy to manage. Exam was fairly easy as long as you review all the content and follow her criteria.
Great professor and pretty easy to be honest.

Chelsea was a fair grader and even let us have a cheat sheet for the final which made it really easy. I did not enjoy the lecture content but she tried her best to make it interesting and engaging. Overall a solid prof.

easy course. dont need to read the readings. just use her power points, she goes everything very clearly. final exam offered a cheat sheet. graded on 2 short essays, participation points from in class worksheet and tutorial, and final exam. light work course. however, we didn't get to know our final grade % as she does not post your grade on canva.

Grading criteria for essays were clear but are marked by TAs so pray you get a lenient TA. Lectures are dry but straightforward summaries of the readings which are appreciated. Final exam was also fair, just study the slides. Overall, good prof, but if you weren't interested in Phil to begin with Chelsea won't change your mind.
Take this course. So easy.

Rosenthal is the best professor I've had this semester. Very understanding and simplifies concepts so they are easy to understand. One of the few teachers I've had that genuinely cares about her students. Hope to take her class again someday.

Good teacher, but talks too much during lectures. Not a big deal if you didn't read the readings for the week as she goes over them during lectures. Weekly discussion assignments are easy and can be done during class, and the papers aren't very hard. Participation during tutorials are good as they can contribute positively to your grade.

Good teacher, very clear with instructions. If you need an upper level elective gpa booster, phil 329 with Chelsea is the class. No need to attend lecture or read any of the readings. Just do a short writing assignment once a month, and two fairly short papers that are easy to write about and thats it. Marks easy too.
Very nice prof and does zoom for lectures that you cant make it to. TAs mark hard but its a W course so papers have to be perfect. Shes a really nice prof.

great prof! very nice and clearly very passionate and knowledgeable about the subject matter! reading heavy but didn't require a textbook which was nice, just articles posted to canvas. very clear criteria. 10/10 would recommend.

Chelsea is one of my favorite phil profs! She is caring, open-minded, and receptive to any contributions in class discussions, so participating is definitely less intimidating. The material might not be for everyone but she still explains them very well. Her lectures were online, but I'm not sure if it'll change to in-person in the future.

Dr. Rosenthal is a kind-hearted and thought-provoking professor who clearly loves what she does. She is regularly accessible during office hours and encourages students to participate in class by posing insightful questions. The papers are both on the longer side, but the content is dense, so it makes sense. I strongly recommend having her!
One of the best profs, very approachable and nice! Would definitely take more classes with her!
She gave out lots of readings but I do not suggest doing them. Her entire lecture are just going over the readings so there is no point in actually doing the readings yourself. Easy class though.

Professor Rosenthal is an amazing prof! Although this is a W course, you don't need to worry too much about the readings. Focus on her lectures and PPT, they are really useful and clear, and you can definitely understand the readings from her lec even you're an international student. We only have one final, and it is basic.

She is honestly amazing, super understanding during these pandemic times and is flexible with assignments. However theres a lot of writing in this class and most is marked by the T.A. Beware half of your grade in the class is based off the T.A marks and tutorials.
Chelsea is a great prof! Her lectures focus on a wide range of topics but she does a good job of making sure her students understand the main points.
